claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: undefined arithmetic, a confirmed Qiskit bug, source patch, production remedy, route improvement, hardware relevance, quantum advantage, BQP separation, solved B4/B8/B10, or new credit
claim_boundary.what_is_supported_if_passed: source-level combine localization on two nonisomorphic exact-tie inputs plus an exact score-level tie/non-tie guardrail

first_divergence_rule.candidate_pair: source_f64 winner and exact_binary64_leaf first-seen winner
first_divergence_rule.chain_binding: unique final combine whose retained leaf-bit vector and result bits match the candidate
first_divergence_rule.first_divergence: first chain event whose result bits differ from the reference bits
first_divergence_rule.native_addition_check: every combine result bits must equal binary64(left + right)
first_divergence_rule.prefix_order: source event order restricted to exact labeled prefixes of the final candidate leaf vector
first_divergence_rule.reference: correctly rounded exact Fraction sum of retained binary64 leaves

policy_under_test.r160_non_tie_requirement: 28/28 unique-minimum mode rows remain strict non-ties
policy_under_test.r160_tie_requirement: 4/4 tie-baseline mode rows remain ties and stable
policy_under_test.r170_r172_requirement: 6/6 exact-tie traces retain the first-seen exact winner
policy_under_test.strict_order: candidate exact Fraction total is lower than incumbent exact Fraction total
policy_under_test.tie_rule: retain first-seen incumbent only when exact Fraction totals are equal

research_question: At which source-level binary64 combine does each R170/R172 exact-tie candidate branch first diverge from the correctly rounded exact prefix, and can an exact-total policy preserve both true ties and declared non-ties?
title: B4/B8/B10 R173 first divergent combine protocol
